message:
Last month our power went out for like six hours, and I'll admit my first instinct was annoyance. Then my kid asked if we could have a "flashlight dinner" in the living room, and suddenly we were all sitting around with our battery-powered lanterns, eating sandwiches and playing card games by lamplight. No screens, no rush—just us.

I realized I'd been stressed about having *no power* when really I had everything I needed: light, food that didn't require cooking, and people I actually wanted to spend time with. We even had phone chargers that still had juice.

Turning it into something cozy totally changed my mood. Sounds simple, but it made me think differently about being ready—less "oh no, disaster" and more "okay, here's what we do."

Have you ever had a power outage that ended up being less bad than you expected?
